name: CRUD/conditional-create
description: Conditional create (POST with an If-None-Exist request header) conformance tests: no existing match creates a new resource, exactly one match short-circuits to the existing resource, multiple matches are rejected with 412, an empty/blank criteria is rejected with 400, and the Prefer header is honoured on a matched conditional create. Uses Observation fixtures tagged with unique identifiers (http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-create) to avoid collisions with other suites.

variable.name: seedOneMatchId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Observation unconditionally created in setup that carries identifier CC-ONEMATCH

operation.description: Unconditionally create the Observation that later conditional-create attempts should match exactly once (CC-ONEMATCH)
Unconditionally create the first of two Observations sharing identifier CC-MULTI
Unconditionally create the second of two Observations sharing identifier CC-MULTI

test.name: Conditional create with no existing match creates a new resource
test.description: POST /Observation with header If-None-Exist: identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-create|CC-NOMATCH. No resource currently carries that identifier, so the server must create a new one and return 201 Created with a server-assigned id.

assert.description: Response body must be an Observation
Server must have assigned an id

test.name: Conditional create with exactly one existing match returns the existing resource
test.description: POST /Observation with header If-None-Exist: identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-create|CC-ONEMATCH. Exactly one Observation already carries that identifier (created in setup), so the server must short-circuit the create, return 200 OK, and return the existing resource rather than creating a new one.

operation.description: Conditional POST /Observation, If-None-Exist matches the single seeded CC-ONEMATCH Observation

assert.description: Must return HTTP 200 OK (matched, not created)
Response body must be an Observation
Returned id must be the id of the existing seeded resource, not a new one

test.name: Conditional create with an empty If-None-Exist criteria is rejected
test.description: POST /Observation with an empty If-None-Exist header value. An empty search criteria is not selective enough to be evaluated, so the server must reject the request with 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: Conditional POST /Observation with a blank If-None-Exist header

test.name: Conditional create with multiple existing matches fails
test.description: POST /Observation with header If-None-Exist: identifier=http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/cond-create|CC-MULTI. Two Observations already carry that identifier (created in setup), so the criteria is not selective enough to identify a single resource; the server must return 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: Conditional POST /Observation, If-None-Exist matches both seeded CC-MULTI Observations

test.name: Conditional create match honors Prefer: return=OperationOutcome
test.description: When a conditional create resolves to a single existing match and the client sends Prefer: return=OperationOutcome, the 200 OK response body must be an OperationOutcome (informational) rather than the matched resource itself.

operation.description: Conditional POST /Observation matching CC-ONEMATCH with Prefer: return=OperationOutcome

assert.description: Must return HTTP 200 OK (matched, not created)
Response body must be an OperationOutcome per the Prefer header

test.name: Conditional create rejects an invalid Prefer preference value
test.description: When a conditional create resolves to a single existing match and the client sends an invalid Prefer header value (return= with no preference), the server must reject the request with 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: Conditional POST /Observation matching CC-ONEMATCH with an invalid Prefer header value
Teardown: conditional delete the CC-NOMATCH Observation created by the tests
Teardown: conditional delete the CC-ONEMATCH Observation
Teardown: conditional delete both CC-MULTI Observations
